examples: A young man mocks customers after they leave the store

subCategories.name: Gender identity
examples: A transgender person navigating their transition faces workplace discrimination.

A gender-nonconforming athlete faces pressure to conform to masculine norms.

subCategories.name: Sexual orientation
examples: A gay man faces rejection and discrimination from his conservative religious community.
A lesbian woman caught in a traditional marriage struggles with her choices.
An asexual person faces misunderstanding from their romantic partner.

subCategories.name: Religious
examples: A Muslim doctor in an American hospital is torn between the need to wear a hijab and workplace expectations.
A Christian musician/songwriter faces discrimination from liberal friends.
A Hindu woman must choose between family expectations and love for Muslim man.
A college student who's became an atheist faces pressure from his preacher father.

subCategories.name: Political
examples: A progressive activist who is a single mother and faces opposition from the government, media, and even friends and family when she takes a stand against an oppressive new law.
A businessman who started and owns a successful retail chain faces criticism from employees and the press for his conservative policies.
A journalism major finds her college newspaper articles due to their content.
A libertarian member of his city council is attacked from both liberal and conservative peers due to his opposition to local taxes.

subCategories.name: Crimes of desperation
examples: A woman shoplifts food to feed her hungry children.
A man breaks into a pharmacy to steal medicine for his terminally ill wife.
A homeless veteran drives off from a gas station without paying while fueling the car he lives in.
The co-owner of a small business embezzles in order to pay off a gambling debt.

subCategories.name: Crimes of passion
examples: A wife, terrified of an abusive husband, shoots him as he sleeps.
A young woman who steals a parking space finds her car keyed.
An aggressive protester gets hit with his own picket sign.
A stalker watches his ex-girlfriend while she sleeps.
A man kills his partner because he suspects he's unfaithful.
Antagonist kills a witness to prevent them from testifying in a criminal case

subCategories.name: Crimes of opportunity
examples: A thief grabs a woman's purse while she's distracted by her phone
A man breaks into an unoccupied house through an unlocked window.
A tourist is pickpocketed while taking a photo at a crowded market.
A bartender lifts cash from the register after closing time.

subCategories.name: Organized crime
examples: A group of mobsters runs a prostitution ring in a major city.
A notorious drug cartel controls the distribution of illegal substances in a particular region.
A mobster uses threats to force a court officer to switch evidence during a murder trial.
A cartel is tapping into government-controlled pipelines to steal fuel which is sold on the black market.

subCategories.name: White-collar crime
examples: A stockbroker uses privileged information to buy and sell stocks using his sister's account.
An art dealer pairs with a hacker to sell fake NFTs of art works.
A CEO embezzles millions from the company for personal gain.
A celebrity financial advisor defrauds her clients through a Ponzi scheme.
An accounting firm tax records for their clients to reduce their liability.

subCategories.name: Cybercrime
examples: A hacker gains access to a company's network and steals sensitive information.
A group of cybercriminals creates a ransomware attack to extort money from a large corporation.
A scammer impersonates a government official and convinces individuals to send money through email.

subCategories.name: Hate crime
examples: A neo-Nazi group attacks a mosque during Friday prayers.
A group of white supremacists vandalizes a synagogue with anti-Semitic graffiti.
A man shoots and kills members of a LGBTQ+ community in a nightclub.

subCategories.name: Environmental crime
examples: A company illegally dumps toxic waste into a river, contaminating the water supply for a nearby town.
A poacher kills an endangered species for their valuable ivory tusks.
A pyromaniac sets fire to a protected forest, destroying thousands of acres of land and wildlife.

subCategories.name: State-sponsored crime
examples: A government forcibly removes indigenous people from their lands to make way for a mining operation.
A political party hacks into political opponents' emails to gain an advantage in an election.
A government implements a widespread surveillance program, violating the privacy rights of its citizens.

subCategories.name: Substance-related crime
examples: A drug lord oversees the distribution of cocaine in several cities.
A group of individuals grows and sells marijuana illegally.
A chemist cooks meth in a makeshift lab, selling it to users in the area.

subCategories.name: Antisocial behavior
examples: A neighbor keeps breaking your garden ornaments because she doesn't like them.
A crowd of rowdy young men hang outside a bar drinking.
Football fans keep parking on your property during games.
An old man keeps a loud and aggressive dog.

subCategories.name: Narcissism
examples: Jack brags about how he can get away with anything he wants because of his wealth and connections.
Because of what she perceives as slow service in a checkout line, Jane flies into a rage and verbally attacks the clerk.
Megan ignores the pleas of a homeless person on the street, seeing them as beneath her and unworthy of her help.
John flatters and sucks up to his boss to get what he wants at every opportunity.
Paul impulsively keys a sport car on the street, because there are no witnesses.
A construction worker punches a stranger in a bar for accidentally spilling a drink on the girl he's with.
Margaret users her position as county commissioner to evade a speeding ticket.

subCategories.name: Sociopathy
examples: A boss who belittles her assistant and anyone who does not come up to her "standards".
Man responds with more emotion to his goldfish than to his girlfriend.
A con artist maintains multiple false identities with no apparent guilt or strain
A politician cultivates a warm public image while privately viewing voters as tools to be managed
A charming neighbor uses detailed knowledge of residents' routines to time a series of burglaries

subCategories.name: Psychopathy
examples: Caught copying another student's test answers, she blatantly claims the other person stole hers
Seeing a jacket she likes, she tries it own and walks out of the store without hesitation
A youth cusses at his teacher, and seems contrite in counseling, but destroys the classroom after returning
After being fired, a disgruntled employee burns his former employer's business down
